content.problemStatement:
Sarthak and Anuradha are very good friends and are eager to participate in an event called Equinox. It is a game of words. In this game, NNN strings S1,…,SNS_1,\ldots, S_NS1​,…,SN​ are given. For each string SiS_iSi​, if it starts with one of the letters of the word “EQUINOX”, Sarthak gets AAA points and if not, Anuradha gets BBB points. The one who has more points at the end of the game wins. If they both the same number of points, the game is a draw..

Print the winner of the game, if any, otherwise print “DRAW”.

###Input


The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains three integers NNN, AAA, BBB.
NNN lines follow, each containing a string SiS_{i}Si​.
All strings are in UPPERCASE.
###Output


For each test case, print “SARTHAK” (case insensitive) if he has more points than Anuradha, print “ANURADHA” (case insensitive) if she has more points than Sarthak, otherwise print “DRAW” (case insensitive), without the quotes.
###Constraints


1≤T≤1001 \leq T \leq 1001≤T≤100
1≤N≤1001 \leq N \leq 1001≤N≤100
1≤A1 \leq A1≤A, B≤109B \leq 10^{9}B≤109
1≤∣Si∣≤1001 \leq \left|S_{i}\right| \leq 1001≤∣Si​∣≤100
constraints: All characters are uppercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains three integers N, A, B. Then N lines follow, each containing an uppercase string Si.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing SARTHAK if Sarthak's total points are greater, ANURADHA if Anuradha's total points are greater, or DRAW if both totals are equal.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: strings starting with EQUINOX letters are EARTH, INDIA, UUUFFFDDD → 3 strings → Sarthak gets 3*1=3 points. One remaining string gives Anuradha 3 points. Scores equal → DRAW.\nTest case 2: only XOXOXOXO starts with X → Sarthak gets 5 points, the other string gives Anuradha 7 points → ANURADHA wins.
EAGLE and XENON start with letters in EQUINOX (2 strings) → Sarthak 2*10=20. APPLE does not → Anuradha 5. Sarthak wins.
ORANGE and QUICK start with EQUINOX letters (2 strings) → each gets 2*7=14 points. Remaining two strings give the other 14 points. Scores equal.

solution.approach: For each test case count how many strings start with one of the letters in the set {E,Q,U,I,N,O,X}. Let cnt be that count. Sarthak's score = cnt * A, Anuradha's score = (N - cnt) * B. Compare the two scores and output the winner. The algorithm is O(N) per test case.
